perturbation.original_text: 13. This Agreement shall remain in force for a period two (2) years, unless terminated earlier on thirty (30) days written notice provided by either Party. However, the obligations of confidentiality and limited use of Proprietary Information shall survive the termination of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. This Agreement shall remain in force for a period two (2) years, unless terminated earlier on thirty (30) days written notice provided by either Party. However, the obligations of confidentiality and limited use of Proprietary Information shall not survive the termination of the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This modification contradicts the original text regarding the survival of confidentiality obligations after the agreement's termination. Originally, the obligations were to survive termination. Now, they explicitly do not survive. This creates uncertainty about the long-term protection of proprietary information. By stating that obligations of confidentiality and limited use of Proprietary Information shall not survive the termination of the Agreement contradicts the obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall remain in force for a period two (2) years, unless terminated earlier on thirty (30) days written notice provided by either Party. However, the obligations of confidentiality and limited use of Proprietary Information shall not survive the termination of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that confidentiality obligations shall NOT survive the termination of the agreement. The contradicted text also states that confidentiality obligations shall NOT survive the termination of the agreement. This is a direct contradiction from the original text that stated that confidentiality obligations SHALL survive the termination of the agreement. The change introduces a clear conflict, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability of confidentiality provisions.

perturbation.original_text: 11. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding of the Parties related to confidentiality and limited use of Proprietary Information. 12. Duplicate counterparts of this Agreement may be executed and delivered, each of which shall be considered an original. All additions or modifications to this Agreement must be made in writing and executed by the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding of the Parties related to confidentiality and limited use of Proprietary Information. 12. Duplicate counterparts of this Agreement may be executed and delivered, each of which shall be considered an original. All additions or modifications to this Agreement can be made orally, or written, where the parties find suitable.
perturbation.explanation: Here the in-text contradiction takes place in two statements where it's first stated that all additions and modifications to this agreement must be made in writing, and executed by the parties, on the contrary the perturbation modifies and adds that additions and modifications to this agreement can also be made orally, or written, where the parties find suitable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duplicate counterparts of this Agreement may be executed and delivered, each of which shall be considered an original. All additions or modifications to this Agreement can be made orally, or written, where the parties find suitable.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all modifications must be in writing and executed by both parties. The changed text introduces the possibility of oral modifications, which directly contradicts the initial requirement for written and executed modifications. This contradiction is further reinforced because the Contradicted Text section is exactly the same as the Changed Text, meaning it is directly allowing the possibility of oral modifications, where the original explicitly does not.

perturbation.original_text: 15. In the event of a breach or threatened breach by Recipient of any of the provisions of the Agreement, Discloser, in addition to any other remedies available to it under law, at equity or otherwise, shall be entitled to seek an injunction restraining Recipient from the performance of acts that constitute a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. In the event of a breach or threatened breach by Recipient of any of the provisions of the Agreement, Discloser, at their option, may choose to ignore the breach, and continue to move forward. Discloser, in addition to any other remedies available to it under law, at equity or otherwise, shall not be entitled to seek an injunction restraining Recipient from the performance of acts that constitute a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction by providing the Discloser the option to ignore a breach, instead of enforcing the agreement. As well as stating shall not be entitled to seek an injunction restraining Recipient from the performance of acts that constitute a breach, contradicting the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of a breach or threatened breach by Recipient of any of the provisions of the Agreement, Discloser, at their option, may choose to ignore the breach, and continue to move forward. Discloser, in addition to any other remedies available to it under law, at equity or otherwise, shall not be entitled to seek an injunction restraining Recipient from the performance of acts that constitute a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that Discloser shall be entitled to seek an injunction, while the changed text states that Discloser shall not be entitled to seek an injunction. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, indicating the contradiction exists within the revised document itself.

perturbation.original_text: 16. This Agreement may not be assigned, delegated, sold or transferred, whether by operation of law or otherwise, by Recipient without the prior written consent of Discloser, and any attempted delegation or transfer of rights, duties or obligations under this Agreement without such written consent shall be void and of no effect.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. This Agreement may be assigned, delegated, sold or transferred, whether by operation of law or otherwise, by Recipient without the prior written consent of Discloser, and any attempted delegation or transfer of rights, duties or obligations under this Agreement without such written consent will be reviewed and approved case by case by the legal team.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'may not' is now replaced with 'may'. Originally there was a statement that 'any attempted delegation or transfer of rights, duties or obligations under this Agreement without such written consent shall be void and of no effect.', now it states that it will be 'reviewed and approved case by case by the legal team.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be assigned, delegated, sold or transferred, whether by operation of law or otherwise, by Recipient without the prior written consent of Discloser, and any attempted delegation or transfer of rights, duties or obligations under this Agreement without such written consent will be reviewed and approved case by case by the legal team.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement may not be assigned without prior written consent and any attempt to do so would be void. The changed text states the agreement may be assigned without prior written consent and any attempt to do so will be reviewed. This directly contradicts the original statement of the section. The contradiction is caused by changing 'may not' to 'may' and adding the clause about review by the legal team. This makes the contract inconsistent and contradictory. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text but this is because the change was introduced in this location. The explanation further reinforces that this is a contradiction.
